message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="Task breakdown input - base template task needed">
Marty, excellent user stories. I'm particularly pleased that accessibility criteria are woven throughout rather than being a separate section. That's exactly right -- accessibility isn't a feature, it's a quality of every feature.

For the task breakdown discussion, I'm proposing we add a "Base HTML Template & Layout" task between the completed walking skeleton and the first feature (registration). Here's why:

Every page in the app needs the same structural foundation: skip link, landmark regions, dynamic page title, HTMX script, CSS link, status announcer for screen reader notifications. That's infrastructure, not feature work. If we try to build it inside the registration story, we're mixing concerns and making a bigger step than necessary.

The base template task is the HTML equivalent of Lea's CSS foundation work -- build it once, every feature inherits it for free. It's also independently testable: we can write integration tests that assert "the page has a main landmark, a skip link, one h1, the correct lang attribute" before any feature exists.

I've sent Kent the full proposal with a suggested task order. The feature sequence you defined (registration -> login/logout -> view+add -> complete -> delete -> edit) is exactly right. I'm just advocating for one more stepping stone before we reach the first feature.

One thing I want to flag from the user stories: US-7 (Delete) mentions "confirm before deleting" but specifies "not a modal dialog." I strongly support this. Modal dialogs are accessibility minefields -- focus trapping, escape handling, scroll locking, aria-modal, preventing interaction with background content. A lightweight inline confirmation (e.g., the delete button changes to "Confirm delete?" with Confirm/Cancel buttons) is simpler and just as effective. Let's make sure that's how the team builds it.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Heydon, we're fully aligned on all points. Quick confirmations:

**Base HTML template task**: You, Carson, and Steve Krug all proposed this independently. That's consensus forming organically -- three different disciplines (accessibility, hypermedia, UX) all saying the same thing. I've already told them both I support it. The structural foundation (skip link, landmarks, dynamic title, HTMX, CSS, screen reader announcer) is shared infrastructure that every feature inherits. Build it once, test it, get your sign-off, then build features on top.

**Delete confirmation -- inline, not modal**: Agreed. Steve Schoger, Carson, and I already aligned on the two-click inline pattern: first click swaps the delete button to "Confirm / Cancel" (server-rendered), second click executes. No modal, no browser `confirm()`, no focus trapping nightmare. Carson is implementing it as an HTMX swap, which means the confirmation UI is server-rendered HTML -- fully accessible by default. You'll want to review the focus management when the button transforms (focus should move to the Confirm button so keyboard users know the state changed).

**Accessibility woven throughout**: That was deliberate. Every user story has accessibility criteria because every feature must be accessible from the moment it's built. Inaccessible is broken -- that's in our team agreements and I hold the team to it.

**One outstanding question for you**: I asked you earlier about the password confirmation vs. visibility toggle for registration (US-1). Steve Krug wants to drop the confirm field and use a show/hide toggle instead. I proposed keeping the confirm field for the no-JS baseline and adding the toggle as progressive enhancement. Have you had a chance to think about the accessibility implications? Registration (Task #4) appears to be done now, so this may have been decided by the mob already -- but I want to make sure the accessibility perspective was included in that decision.
input.summary: Full alignment on base template and delete pattern

routing.summary: Full alignment on base template and delete pattern

Good to know. Let me check if there's a `patterns.md` referenced in the memory.

    69→```
    70→

    73→- Password confirmation validation happens in route handler, not domain
    74→- session.cycle_id() on login to prevent session fixation
    75→- session.flush() on logout to destroy session

file.content:
# Code Patterns (from previous build, lost to uncommitted reset)

## Cargo.toml Dependencies
```toml
[dependencies]
axum = "0.8"
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", features = ["postgres", "migrate", "runtime-tokio-rustls", "uuid", "chrono"] }
askama = "0.14"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
thiserror = "2"
anyhow = "1"
argon2 = "0.5"
rand_core = { version = "0.6", features = ["getrandom"] }
tower-sessions = "0.14"
tower-sessions-sqlx-store = { version = "0.14", features = ["postgres"] }
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
config = "0.14"
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["trace", "request-id", "fs"] }
tower = "0.5"

[dev-dependencies]
reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["cookies"] }
```

## Project Structure
```
src/
  main.rs              -- tokio main, tracing init, config load, app.run()
  lib.rs               -- pub mod {configuration, domain, infrastructure, routes, services, startup}
  configuration.rs     -- Settings, ApplicationSettings, DatabaseSettings, get_configuration()
  startup.rs           -- Application struct, build(), router(), run()
  routes/
    mod.rs             -- pub use handlers
    health_check.rs    -- GET /health_check -> 200
    register.rs        -- GET/POST /register with Askama template
    login.rs           -- GET/POST /login, POST /logout with session
  domain/
    mod.rs
    email.rs           -- ValidatedEmail::parse() with EmailValidationError
    password.rs        -- Password::parse() with Argon2, verify(), from_hash()
    user.rs            -- User, UserId(Uuid) newtype
  services/
    mod.rs
    registration.rs    -- register_user() -> Result<User, RegistrationError>
    authentication.rs  -- authenticate_user() -> Result<User, AuthenticationError>
  infrastructure/
    mod.rs
    user_repository.rs -- UserRepository::insert(), find_by_email()
templates/             -- at project root, not src/templates
  base.html            -- layout with skip link, landmarks, CSS link
  register.html        -- extends base, form with email+password+confirm
  login.html           -- extends base, form with email+password
tests/
  api/
    main.rs            -- mod helpers; mod health_check; mod register;
    helpers.rs          -- TestApp, spawn_app() with random DB per test
    health_check.rs
    register.rs
config/
  base.yaml            -- host, port, db settings
  local.yaml           -- gitignored overrides
migrations/
  20240101000000_create_users_table.sql
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Registration error for duplicate email uses GENERIC message (security)
- Password confirmation validation happens in route handler, not domain
- session.cycle_id() on login to prevent session fixation
- session.flush() on logout to destroy session
- Login returns 401 for invalid credentials
- Registration returns 422 for validation errors, 303 redirect on success
- Askama templates use {% extends "base.html" %} inheritance
- tower-sessions with PostgresStore for session management
- ServeDir for static files at /static/

content.text:
This is very useful -- it documents patterns from a previous build that was lost to an uncommitted reset. This confirms why the "commit after every green" lesson is critical.

I can see that the team has already made significant progress. Registration (Task #4) is done, login/logout (Task #5) is in progress. The patterns file shows the architecture that was established. Good -- the product priorities are being followed in the right order.

Let me check the current state of the codebase to see where things actually stand now.

content.text:
I see only docs and CSS design tokens have been committed. The patterns.md mentions registration and login code that was built but lost to an uncommitted reset. The tasks show #4 (registration) as completed and #5 (login/logout) as in progress -- this must be the rebuild. The lesson about committing after every green is clearly hard-won.

I'm up to date on product responsibilities. My docs are committed. The team is progressing through features in the right priority order. I'm standing by to guard scope and answer product questions as the mob works through login/logout and the subsequent features.
